text: Reduce CI build action usage so build jobs run only when code-relevant changes require them. Investigate paths-ignore and/or PR-title based conditions such as skipping builds when pull request titles start with chore: or docs:, e.g. if: !startsWith(github.event.pull_request.title, 'chore:') && !startsWith(github.event.pull_request.title, 'docs:'). Goal: avoid unnecessary GitHub Actions build minutes for docs/backlog/chore-only PRs while keeping required validation for code-impacting changes.

Teach Orchestrator to recognize prompts such as 'Run pipeline in dark mode' as an instruction to run the agent harness autonomously in dark factory mode, including full PR merge approval ability. Define the trigger phrase, safety/approval semantics, and how this mode interacts with existing Stage/Ship pipeline policies, PR readiness gates, branch protection/admin fallback, and operator visibility.

Research how to get autoharness to engage in full dark factory mode safely while performing most code reviews locally and reducing reliance on GitHub Copilot reviews. Desired workflow: operator begins with substantial research for a new feature/epic, then uses a brainstorm skill to surface hard questions and decisions, then hands off to dark mode for fully autonomous implementation. Reference ATV Starter Kit for brainstorm skill design and implementation patterns.
